item.title: I need to brag about this
item.description: I've been overweight for more than 18 years and I lost about 40kg the past 3 years. It's not enough to be at a "normal" range (still overweight) but enough to start trying to run safely and it's a real victory for someone who, if you'd asked me 3 years ago that I started to like running, would've laughed you in the face for it. I don't care if this dies in the usersub, I'm feeling good right now. ヾ(⌐■_■)ノ♪ (๑•̀ㅂ•́)و✧ (~‾▿‾)~ Edit: In freedom units: 9km per hour = 5,6 miles per hour, 40kg = 88 pounds
item.dc:creator: Sidaige

item.title: Would you like to see some adorable otters?
item.description:
That was a rhetorical question. Because of course you want to see some adorable otters.
BABY OTTER SWIM TIME ENRICHMENT
Want to play, but so sleepy....
Toy distraction: 60% of the time, it works every time.
This little baby was just rescued very recently:
These are from the VA Marine Mammal Rescue Society on IG: https://www.instagram.com/vammrs If you have the means, I encourage you to support them in any way you can! ❤️

This has basically been me all day

oh to be dressed as a clown committing crimes
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream (m/w/f around 1830 UK time) http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: Weird Al Yankovic just dropped a new animated music video, "Polkamania!"
item.description:
Weird Al's newest single is a polka medley parodying hit songs of the last 10 years. The animators included: Ghostbot / Alan Lau, Roque Ballesteros, and Roman Laney – “Flowers” Jack D. Evans – “We Don’t Talk About Bruno” Vivienne Medrano/Spindlehorse – “Vampire” Liam Lynch – “Old Town Road” Jarrett Heather – “Despacito” & “WAP” Bill Plympton – “Shape of You” Ryan Krzak – “Uptown Funk” KukoMitzu – “Thank U, Next” Victor Yerrid & Melanie Mandl – “Shake It Off (pt. 1)”
Source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y0ZoX4dBvwk
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.dc:creator: MissySassy

item.title: Weight loss journey
item.description: I've still got a lot to lose... I had an old Weight Watchers scale and after about a year of it reading E I decided to get serious about losing weight and bought a new scale that I knew couldn't possibly error out because its limit was way higher than what I suspected my weight was close to. My weight ended up being about 30 pounds over the limit of the WW scale. I'm now 10 under that limit. I still have another 50 pounds to go before my old wardrobe will fit, and I'll be back to my old "thicc but not obese" self. I haven't actually been working out or anything, that never works for me. Instead I've been monitoring my carb intake and mostly doing a high protein, high fat diet, reducing my sugar intake. My diet mainly consists of two meals a day. A sausage egg and cheese sandwich on a low carb hamburger bun, and dinner is either a cheeseburger with lettuce and caramelized onion or a "sloppy chop cheese" which is taking the beef and crumble cooking it with chopped onions and some cheese to make it closely resemble a Philly chopped cheese, and on one of those low carb hamburger buns. Actual physical activity has not really increased an appreciable amount. I've generally found that working out plateaus the weight loss after about 20 pounds or so, and then I have to keep exercising the same amount or I gain it back again. Rather than put myself through that hell, I'm just doing what actually works for me: adjusting my diet and getting used to smaller portions. I'm not keeping track of how many times I cheat on the diet. Sometimes I eat more than usual, mostly socially because restaurants have huge portions, and I've gotten ice cream a few times this month because of the heat, but only after being out in the heat for a long freaking time. TL;DR Low stress, high effectiveness diet that I've created specifically for me.
item.dc:creator: BerryBnuuy
